title: Psychology Program Instructional Objectives
content:
Psychology Program Instructional Objectives

PLO1: Demonstrate a comprehensive understanding of the fundamental scientific principles, concepts, and theoretical frameworks of psychology.

PLO2: Develop proficiency in applying the scientific method, utilizing statistical analysis techniques, and designing research studies; demonstrate the ability to plan, conduct, and report psychological research projects effectively.

PLO3: Acquire the skills necessary to assess, observe, and design interventions for behavioral and cognitive processes through the laboratory, clinical, and health-related practice opportunities provided by the university.

PLO4: Exhibit critical and analytical thinking abilities, embrace a scientific and evidence-based approach, and act in accordance with the ethical standards and professional values of psychology.

PLO5: Communicate effectively in both written and oral forms, and demonstrate openness and competence in interdisciplinary collaboration within academic and professional contexts.
